title: A resolution expressing the sense of the Senate that the Russian Federation started the war against Ukraine by launching an unprovoked full-scale invasion of Ukraine on February 24, 2022.
plain_summary: This Senate resolution says that Russia started the war with Ukraine by attacking the country without any good reason on February 24, 2022. The Senate believes Russia had already done illegal things to Ukraine back in 2014 before this bigger attack happened. The resolution makes it clear that the Senate thinks Russia is to blame for starting this war. It officially states the Senate's position that Russia's invasion was wrong and unprovoked.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Foreign Relations. (text: CR S1584)

sections.title: Resolution
sections.text: The Senate believes that Russia started the war against Ukraine by launching an unprovoked full-scale invasion of Ukraine on February twenty-fourth, twenty twenty-two. This invasion happened after Russia illegally took over Crimea (a region of Ukraine) in twenty fourteen and illegally occupied parts of the Donbas region (another area in eastern Ukraine) in twenty fourteen.
